name: Kettlebell Bottoms-Up Carry
resistance_profile: Holding a kettlebell upside-down forces the wrist flexors and grip to work isometrically to keep the bell balanced above the handle while you walk.
cues: Wrist straight and stacked under the bell
execution_steps: Clean or curl a kettlebell to the rack position and balance it so the bell sits directly above the handle.
Grip the handle hard and keep the wrist straight and stacked under the bell.
Brace the core and walk a set distance, keeping the bell balanced and vertical.
Set it down, switch hands, and repeat on the other side.
good_when: You want to build crushing grip strength and wrist stability
You want a forearm drill that also challenges shoulder stability and the core
bad_when: You have no clear floor space to walk
Wrist or shoulder pain makes the unstable load risky
common_errors: Letting the wrist bend so the bell tips over
Holding the handle with a loose grip
progressions: Carry for a longer distance or time
regressions: Perform a stationary bottoms-up hold instead of walking
